name: wikipedia
description: Broad public Wikipedia research across search, pages, links, categories, history, languages, geography, users, and random discovery.

runtime.auth_interaction: one normal Wikipedia login when an account action first needs it

typed_fast_paths: bounded read and write access to the MediaWiki Action API
coverage.website_parity: After one normal login or signup authorization, AgentWeb reuses the retained session and obtains MediaWiki CSRF tokens automatically. api_request covers Action API reads and mutations, upload handles multipart media, and the direct protocol surface covers REST and newly introduced same-site operations.
human_boundaries: password, CAPTCHA, email verification, and terms consent during account authorization
constraints: MediaWiki permissions and abuse filters still apply
CAPTCHA and account verification require a visible human handoff

search.description: Search Wikipedia article titles and text with compact snippets.
search.when_to_use: Use this to find article titles from keywords when the exact page title is unknown.
search.when_not_to_use: Do not use this when the exact article title is already known; call page instead.

language.description: Wikipedia language code, such as en or hi

page.description: Read a Wikipedia page as bounded plain text with canonical metadata.
page.when_to_use: Use this to read one article's bounded plain-text extract and canonical metadata.
page.when_not_to_use: Do not use this to discover articles from keywords; call search first.

random.description: Return random article titles and links from a Wikipedia language edition.
random.when_to_use: Use this to sample random main-namespace articles for exploration or testing.
random.when_not_to_use: Do not use this when a specific topic or title is required.

backlinks.when_to_use: Use this to find which articles link to a page (its incoming links).

pageviews.description: Return daily public pageviews and a total for a date range.
pageviews.when_to_use: Use this to get daily pageview counts and a total for an article over a date range.
pageviews.when_not_to_use: Do not use hyphenated dates; the range must be eight-digit YYYYMMDD values.

start.description: Eight-digit YYYYMMDD date, for example 20260701; hyphens are not accepted.

end.description: Eight-digit YYYYMMDD date, for example 20260731; hyphens are not accepted.

api_query.description: Bounded read-only escape hatch for MediaWiki query, parse, opensearch, and help actions. Write actions are rejected.
api_query.when_to_use: Use this read-only escape hatch when a typed command does not cover a MediaWiki read.
api_query.when_not_to_use: Do not use this for mutations; write actions are rejected. Use api_request with confirm.

parameters.description: A normal JSON object of MediaWiki parameters, such as {"action":"opensearch","search":"Alan Turing"}. A legacy array of key=value strings is also accepted.

max_items.description: Maximum list items retained in the response

max_string.description: Maximum characters kept per string value

max_total_chars.description: Hard ceiling for the complete nested JSON response

errors.message: api_query permits only read-only query, parse, opensearch, or help actions.
errors.user_action: Remove write actions and use a read-only MediaWiki action.

errors.message: The MediaWiki Action API rejected the request or reported an error.
errors.user_action: Read the returned error text, correct the parameters, and retry.

account_status.description: Verify the retained Wikipedia session and report the authenticated user, groups, rights, and block state.
account_status.when_to_use: Use this to check whether a Wikipedia session is authenticated and its groups and rights.
account_status.when_not_to_use: Do not use this to inspect another user; call user_contributions.

edit.description: Create or replace a Wikipedia page through the MediaWiki Action API. AgentWeb fetches the CSRF token automatically; confirm is mandatory.
edit.when_to_use: Use this to create or replace a page's wikitext once a session is authorized; confirm is mandatory.
edit.when_not_to_use: Do not use this without confirm=true, and prefer a sandbox page when testing.

text.description: Full replacement wikitext for the page or section

summary.description: Short edit summary shown in the page history

section.description: Optional section index or 'new' to append a section

confirm.description: Must be true to perform the state-changing edit

errors.message: This operation changes remote state and requires confirm=true.
errors.user_action: Inspect the inputs and repeat the call with confirm set to true.

errors.message: This operation needs an authenticated Wikipedia session.
errors.user_action: Run agentweb connect wikipedia to authorize a session once, then retry.

api_request.description: Call a fixed-host MediaWiki Action API operation. Read actions use GET; mutations use POST, require confirm, reuse the retained login session, and fetch a fresh CSRF token automatically.
api_request.when_to_use: Use this fixed-host Action API bridge for reads, or for writes with confirm and a retained login.
api_request.when_not_to_use: Do not use this for typed reads that already have a dedicated command.

confirm.description: Must be true for write actions that change remote state

max_items.description: Maximum list items retained in the response

max_string.description: Maximum characters kept per string value

max_total_chars.description: Hard ceiling for the complete nested JSON response

upload.description: Upload a local file through MediaWiki's multipart Action API. AgentWeb fetches the CSRF token automatically and never returns the local path; confirm is mandatory.
upload.when_to_use: Use this to upload a local media file through MediaWiki's multipart API; confirm is mandatory.
upload.when_not_to_use: Do not use this without confirm=true; uploads are hard to reverse and need file rights.

path.description: Local file path to upload; the path is never echoed back

ignore_warnings.description: Proceed even if MediaWiki reports non-fatal warnings

confirm.description: Must be true to perform the upload
